The classification of Artabotrys corniculatus includes its placement in the order Magnoliales and the clade Magnoliids. intro: Artabotrys corniculatus, also known as the beaded fruit vine or forest apricot, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Artabotrys corniculatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Artabotrys corniculatus is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal formulations, such as capsules and tinctures.
